attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who accumulates massive kilometers in breaks (500+ km in single Grand Tour), then executes tactical positioning to force rivals into leadout work before stealing sprint in final kilometer.
team_tactics: Operates with high individual autonomy in breakaways; minimal domestique support visible, relying on personal endurance reserves and tactical cunning to exploit rivals' fatigue in finale.
terrain_preference: Long-distance breakaway stages where exceptional endurance capacity becomes decisive advantage; excels when peloton miscalculates chase dynamics allowing break survival.
vulnerability: Single-race sample limits assessment, but reliance on peloton miscalculation suggests weakness when sprinter teams execute disciplined chase; may struggle when forced to initiate sprint rather than position tactically.
key_patterns: Exceptional endurance: accumulated 500+ km in breakaways across single Grand Tour
Tactical positioning expert: forced rival to lead out inside 1km then stole sprint
Exploits peloton miscalculation: won when sprinter teams misjudged gap by 25 meters
form_trajectory: unknown: only single race observation from 2019 Giro stage win; demonstrated exceptional endurance and tactical acumen but insufficient data for trajectory assessment
